claims.statement: A solar reference reproduction run requires solver metadata and hashes before it can be treated as solver-backed.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: MESA solver context for stellar model reproduction metadata.

claims.statement: Fixture-only solar reference runs must not be described as external solver reproduction.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: External reproduction requires solver artifacts, not fixtures.

claims.statement: MESA-style solar profile reproduction provides stellar-structure context for profile validation and benchmark handoff.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: Reference for MESA stellar evolution/profile context.

claims.statement: Solar pp-chain and CNO microphysics context should be citation-backed when interpreting solar fusion closure.

sources.citation: Adelberger, E. G. et al. (2011). Solar fusion cross sections II: the pp chain and CNO cycles.
sources.note: Reference for pp-chain and CNO-cycle reaction-rate context.

claims.statement: Borexino-style solar neutrino closure provides observational context for solar-core fusion diagnostics.

sources.citation: Borexino Collaboration (2018). Comprehensive measurement of pp-chain solar neutrinos.
sources.note: Reference for pp-chain solar neutrino observational closure.

claims.statement: GYRE-style solar oscillation summaries can provide optional asteroseismic closure context for solar reference runs.

sources.citation: Townsend, R. H. D. and Teitler, S. A. (2013). GYRE: an open-source stellar oscillation code.

claims.statement: Solar reference reproduction can improve StarSim stellar-microphysics fidelity but is not direct ER=EPR evidence.

sources.citation: Maldacena, J. and Susskind, L. (2013). Cool horizons for entangled black holes.
sources.note: ER=EPR conjectural context; not a stellar fusion evidence claim.

claims.statement: Solar reference run outputs remain proxy-only QST context and cannot promote CL0-CL4 claims.

sources.citation: Carcassi, G., Landini, G. and Aidala, C. (2024). Classical mechanics as the high-entropy limit of quantum mechanics.
sources.note: Supports entropy/ensemble guardrail framing for QST-adjacent claims.

claims.statement: hSpectralFit in solar reference reports remains calibration-only and cannot claim a new Planck constant.

sources.equation: h = 6.62607015e-34 J s exactly in the revised SI
sources.note: Reference for exact Planck constant value.
